sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image:
- Green at (19, 30) is selected (dark border)
- Cross appears at (19, 36) - to the right, at the position of the other green!
So if I click this cross, the green at (19, 30) will move to (19, 36), consuming the green there. After this, I'd have 3 greens: one at (19, 36) and the two at (25, 42) and (37, 42).
Wait, the cross is AT the position of the green (19, 36). So clicking it would move the selected green to that position, consuming the green there. The result would be 1 green at (19, 36) (the moving one) plus the 2 bottom ones = 3 total.
Let me think about the goal pattern. Current greens:
- (19, 30), (19, 36) - top panel
- (25, 42), (37, 42) - bottom panel
If I consume (19, 36) by moving (19, 30) there:
- (19, 36) - top panel (merged)
- (25, 42), (37, 42) - bottom panel
Then if I select (19, 36) and the cross appears at one of the bottom positions, I could move it there, consuming one of them. That would leave 2 greens.
But what's the goal? Let me think about it differently. Maybe the goal is to have exactly 2 greens remaining, one in each panel? Or maybe the goal is to have them all in one panel?
Actually, I notice something. The bottom panel greens are at (25, 42) and (37, 42), both in column 42. The top panel has greens at (19, 30) and (19, 36), both in row 19. 
What if the goal is to have all greens in column 42? That would mean moving the top panel greens to column 42. But the cross only appears in directions where there are other greens to consume. When I selected (19, 30), the cross appeared at (19, 36), not at (19, 42). This suggests the cross appears at the nearest green in that direction.
Let me just continue consuming and see what happens. I'll click the cross at (19, 36) to consume that green.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Selected (19,30), cross at (19,36) - will consume that green. 4 greens remain.
Plan: Click cross to consume (19,36). Then continue reducing greens.
